rag_rules: Fix unresolved references in AGREE contracts by ensuring all identifiers are valid AADL features or declared helpers in the same annex.
Avoid placing guarantee statements in implementation scope; use assign only for output features or declared auxiliary variables.
Do not introduce package-qualified identifiers in AGREE code that are not present in the model's configured dependencies.
generic_rules: When generating AGREE contracts, validate that all referenced identifiers resolve to valid AADL features or declared constants/expressions.
Use only visible AADL features or declared helpers in the same annex for AGREE expressions to prevent 'named thing must be an expression with a type' errors.
Ensure AGREE scalar type declarations match AADL conventions: int, real, bool.
Align helper declaration types with the types of their expressions to prevent assumed vs actual type mismatches.
syntax_or_pattern_reminders: AGREE contracts must be enclosed in `annex agree {** ... **};` blocks.
Component type annexes are for assumptions/guarantees; implementation annexes are for bindings and assignments.
Assignments in implementation scope must target existing output features or declared auxiliary variables.
AGREE expressions must reference only identifiers visible in the same annex or declared as constants/helpers.
anti_patterns: Do not add new architecture elements to fix AGREE diagnostics; instead, resolve references or remove invalid clauses.
Avoid placing guarantee statements in implementation scope; use assign for output bindings only.
Do not introduce package-qualified identifiers unless they are already present in the model's dependencies.
